title: BasicAuth encodes username:password incorrectly when username contains a colon
description: auth.rs BasicAuth::auth_header(): format\!('{}:{}', username, password) is base64-encoded and sent as Basic credentials. RFC 7617 §2 forbids colons in user-ids, but the implementation does not validate or reject them. A caller who passes username='admin:root' and password='pass' produces base64('admin:root:pass'), which the server decodes as user='admin', password='root:pass' — silently sending the wrong credentials with no error. The code comment claims RFC 7617 compliance but the constraint is not enforced.
